title: Historian Thoroughness Rubric
description: Shape enforced (by src/lib/historian/eval/rubric.ts's hand-rolled validator — see that file's header comment for why this is documentation rather than an ajv-driven contract) for every rubric file under qa/historian-eval/rubric/. One 'base' file (base-neuro-hpi.json, syndrome: null) applies to every session; syndromes/*.json files layer syndrome-specific critical questions on top, selected by src/lib/historian/eval/rubric.ts's loadRubric().

_note.description: Provenance disclaimer. Every rubric file ships with the literal string '[training knowledge — Steve to vet]' until a clinician signs off (see vetted_by).

version.description: Stable identifier for this rubric file's content, e.g. 'stroke-v1' or 'base-neuro-hpi-v1'. Unique across every rubric file (base + all syndromes). Append-only: once used in a persisted historian_evaluations.rubric_version, never edit or reuse the id — bump to -v2 instead.

syndrome.description: Syndrome id this rubric targets, matching a tests/simulated-patients/personas/*.json basename — or null for the base (syndrome-agnostic) rubric.

vetted_by.description: Clinician name/credential once reviewed (e.g. 'Steve Arbogast, DO'), or null before vetting. thoroughnessJudge.ts sets ThoroughnessEvaluation.unvetted = true whenever any rubric contributing to a scoring pass has vetted_by === null.

vetted_date.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) of vetting, or null before vetting.

critical_questions.description: The must-ask checklist for this rubric file. Despite the field name (kept verbatim from the task spec), entries carry a per-item severity — not every entry is severity:'critical'. thoroughnessJudge.ts's judge prompt must not award an overall score above 85 if any severity:'critical' item here was never asked/covered in the transcript.

id.description: Stable short identifier, unique within this file, referenced verbatim by the judge's missed_critical_questions[].rubric_id.

question.description: Plain-language description of what must be asked/covered (not necessarily verbatim wording the historian must use).

expected_dimensions.description: Which of the judge's 6 fixed scoring dimensions this rubric file's critical_questions meaningfully bear on. Documentation/audit metadata — the judge always scores all 6 dimensions regardless of this list.
